'''Krystian Mapapalangi''' (born 4 November 2002) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Mapapalangi was born in [[Burwood, New South Wales]]. He is of [[Demographics of Tonga|Tongan]] descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/03/15/where-were-from-our-cultural-heritage/|title=Where we're from: Our cultural heritage|via=www.newcastleknights.com.au}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early years===
Mapapalangi played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]' [[Harold Matthews Cup]] team in 2018 and the [[S. G. Ball Cup]] side in 2019.<ref name="auto1">{{Cite web|url=https://18thman.com/players/krystian-mapapalangi/|title=Krystian Mapapalangi|date=13 March 2022}}</ref> In 2020, he joined the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]]' S. G. Ball Cup team.<ref name="auto1"/> Mid-way through 2021, he joined the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2022/08/16/knights-v-raiders-round-23-nrl-team-list/|title=Knights v Raiders Round 23 NRL team list|date=16 August 2022|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===2022===
Ahead of the 2022 season, Mapapalangi signed a 2-year contract to be a development player with the Knights' [[National Rugby League|NRL]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/11/10/2022-signings-tracker-official-rosters-for-all-16-clubs/|title=2021 signings tracker: Contracts by club|date=24 January 2022|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> In August, his contract was extended until the end of 2024 and upgraded into the top 30 squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/01/27/2022-signings-tracker-official-rosters-for-all-17-nrl-clubs/|title=2022 NRL Signings Tracker: Hunt extends at Sharks; Amone locked in with Red V|date=16 August 2022|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> In round 23 of the [[2022 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut for the Knights against the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref name="auto"/>

===2024===
Mapapalangi played 4 matches for the Knights in 2024, after not playing any throughout 2023. He parted ways with the club at the end of the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/knights-confirm-departures-of-six-players-214405/|title=Knights confirm departures of six players|first=Ethan Lee|last=Chalk|date=17 September 2024|website=Zero Tackle}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 2 April, Mapapalangi had signed a train and trial contract with the Wests Tigers.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-04-02 |title=Wests Tigers NRL roster update |url=https://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2025/04/02/wests-tigers-nrl-roster-update/ |access-date=2025-04-02 |website=Wests Tigers |language=en}}</ref>

===2026===
On 22 January, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Mapapalangi on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=22 January 2026 |title=Castleford sign Wests Tigers centre Mapapalangi |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4g33pn997zo |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=22 January 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers land NRL centre in latest major recruitment move |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-land-nrl-centre |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He made his debut against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 February 2026 |title=Doncaster 0 Castleford Tigers 14: Not picture perfect but job done for Ryan Carr |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-0-castleford-tigers-14-not-picture-perfect-but-job-done-for-ryan-carr-5507339 |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> and was named Tigers Man of the Match in the opening round defeat against [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=20 February 2026 |title=Culture change lifting Castleford Tigers as 'good pick up' shows way forward |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/culture-change-lifting-castleford-tigers-as-good-pick-up-shows-way-forward-5605182 |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Lanigan |first=Adam |date=15 February 2026 |title=Wigan start with hard-fought win at Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c363nd47400o |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Walker |first=Callum |date=19 February 2026 |title=Krystian Mapapalangi gives honest account of Super League debut as Castleford fans hailed |url=https://www.totalrl.com/krystian-mapapalangi-gives-honest-account-of-super-league-debut-as-castleford-fans-hailed/ |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> In round 2, he scored his first try and provided an assist for his winger [[Jason Qareqare]] against [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cresswell |first=Matthew |date=21 February 2026 |title=Toulouse maintain strong start in win over Castleford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2dj76p527o |access-date=21 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Mapapalangi failed a head injury assessment against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the cup and was thus ruled out for round 5, due to a late high tackle for which [[Kyle Feldt]] received a three-match ban.<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 March 2026 |title=St Helens winger Feldt given three-game ban |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cz9qd3kplxqo |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He provided three try assists for Qareqare against [[Bradford Bulls]] in round 6, earning praise for a "sublime performance".<ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=27 March 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers have unearthed a new Super League star |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-have-unearth-a-new-super-league-star |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> After scoring two tries to lead Castleford to victory against Wigan in round 8, head coach [[Ryan Carr (rugby league)|Ryan Carr]] reiterated his hope to agree a long-term contract with Mapapalangi.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=20 April 2026 |title=Castleford coach waxes lyrical over Krystian Mapapalangi as contract latest delivered after Wigan win |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ryan-carr-krystian-mapapalangi-praise-20-04-2026 |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=20 April 2026 |title=Super League warned over Krystian Mapapalangi as Castleford make contract priority clear |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/super-league-warned-krystian-mapapalangi-1798707 |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=21 April 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers have long term plan for star signing Krystian Mapapalangi |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-have-long-term-plan-for-star-signing-krystian-mapapalangi-6972080 |access-date=4 May 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref>
